mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows month-over-month growth exceeding 0.3%, the Fed faces a policy bind. Real rates turn deeply negative, compressing equity risk premiums and driving S&P 500 forward returns positive as investors rotate into equities as inflation hedges.
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2022-01-15, FEDFUNDS=0.38% and CPIAUCSL=239.927 indicating negative real rates
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC falls below 0.05 over any 18-month rolling window during the trigger regime.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 5.0% signals tight labor markets and wage pressure, which equity investors interpret as evidence of robust demand and earnings growth. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns as the market prices in stronger corporate profitability.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=4.9% as of 2022-01-15, already below 5.0% threshold
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns when UNRATE < 5.0% are not significantly positive (mean return < 1.5% or t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) month-over-month signals weakening consumer demand and signals the onset of an economic slowdown. Equity investors reduce growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns as the market reprices earnings downward.
regime_hint: demand deceleration or early recession signal
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC magnitude remains below 0.06 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.2% month-over-month while remaining positive, indicating supply-side weakness without demand collapse. This regime produces negative equity returns as investors fear stagflation: persistent inflation with slowing growth erodes real earnings.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth 0% to 0.2% AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 5%
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns in the stagflation trigger regime exceed +2% on average, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window during periods matching the trigger condition.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate above 1200 thousand units annualized, signaling strong residential investment and forward-looking demand. This leads to positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices cyclical growth expectations upward and construction-related equity sectors outperform.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=1164.0 thousand units as of 2022-01-15, just below trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100 thousand new jobs per month while unemployment remains stable, signaling labor market cooling without shock. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors interpret slowing job creation as a leading indicator of demand weakness.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 100 thousand AND UNRATE stable (change < 0.2pp)
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC magnitude < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate rises above 1.0% in a single month (signaling accelerated tightening), while real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains below 2% annualized. This policy-growth mismatch produces negative S&P 500 returns as the market prices in recession risk from aggressive rate hikes into slowing growth.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS month-over-month increase > 0.6pp AND GDPC1 annualized growth < 2%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.38% and GDPC1=16514.6 billion as of 2022-01-15
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with slowing growth
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns exceed +1.5% on average when trigger is active, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month while payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth remains robust (> 150 thousand), indicating synchronized supply and labor expansion. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns as the market prices in sustained earnings growth without stagflation risk.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 150 thousand
regime_hint: synchronized supply-labor expansion without inflation shock
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 18-month rolling window during synchronized expansion regimes.
